text.title: performance-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for performance-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Running a load test without a committed baseline.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a baseline, there is no definition of regression. Every result is the current state with no directional signal.

failure-mode.description: Skipping warm-up: recording metrics from the first 60 seconds of a run.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: First-run results include JIT compilation, cold cache fills, and DB query planner stats. They are not representative of steady-state performance and inflate tail latency figures.

failure-mode.description: Running cProfile or a deterministic profiler concurrently with a load test.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Deterministic profilers add 10-30% overhead, distorting both the load test results and the profile. Profile separately under a low-concurrency synthetic load.

failure-mode.description: Testing only on the local machine instead of a staging environment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Local machines have different CPU governor, network latency, and I/O characteristics. Results are not portable to production. Conclusions drawn from local runs routinely fail to reproduce on staging.

failure-mode.description: LLM agent reports "fast" based on a single 5-second run.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A single run has no statistical validity. Require N greater than or equal to 3 runs, report mean ± stdev, and assert stdev / mean less than 0.1 before accepting a result as stable.

failure-mode.description: Using time.time() instead of time.perf_counter() for sub-millisecond timing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: time.time() is not monotonic and has platform-dependent resolution (up to 10 ms on some systems). time.perf_counter() is monotonic and high-resolution.

failure-mode.description: Async benchmark awaiting tasks sequentially instead of concurrently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents generate sequential await chains in async benchmarks, testing serialized latency instead of concurrent throughput. Use asyncio.gather() to simulate real concurrent load.
